title: EdhocInitiator/EdhocResponder can be used after export_oscore without reset
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs

After calling export_oscore(), the EdhocInitiator/EdhocResponder instances remain usable but in an inconsistent state:
- Ephemeral secret is already consumed (eph_secret = None)
- PRK values still exist in memory
- Calling methods again could produce undefined behavior

For EdhocInitiator:
- create_message_1() would work (doesn't use eph_secret)
- process_message_2() would fail with InvalidState (eph_secret consumed)

For EdhocResponder:
- process_message_1() would fail with InvalidState (eph_secret consumed)

The state machine is partially protected by eph_secret consumption, but:
1. create_message_1 can be called multiple times before process_message_2
2. export_oscore can be called multiple times (returns same context)

Impact: Potential for confused state if callers don't understand the protocol flow.

Fix: Add explicit state enum (Created, Msg1Sent, Msg2Processed, Complete) and check state before each operation. Consider consuming self in export_oscore:
```rust
pub fn export_oscore(self) -> Result<Context, OscoreError>
```
